text: When building a website, security is essential to protect both users and data. First, use HTTPS to encrypt information sent between the browser and the server. This prevents attackers from intercepting sensitive details like passwords or credit card numbers. Authentication is another key layer, ensuring only authorised users can access private areas. This often involves strong passwords or multi-factor login. Input validation is equally important: always check and clean any data submitted through forms. If not validated, malicious code could be injected into your site. Common vulnerabilities include SQL injection and cross-site scripting (XSS). These can be avoided by using prepared statements and escaping user input. Keeping software updated and limiting user permissions also greatly reduces risk.
